mission.title: Install and Configure Armada on Kubernetes
mission.description: Armada is a multi-cluster batch queuing system designed for high-throughput workloads on Kubernetes. It enables efficient scheduling and management of jobs across multiple clusters, making it ideal for enterprises with large compute needs.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the G-Research Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the Armada chart.
```bash
helm repo add g-research https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Armada using Helm
steps.description:
Install Armada in the desired namespace (e.g., 'armada') with the specified version.
```bash
helm install armada g-research/armada --version 0.20.36 --namespace armada --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the Armada pods to ensure they are running correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Armada deployment to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment armada --namespace armada -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"armada","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"1000m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Set up Prometheus monitoring
steps.description:
Ensure that Prometheus is configured to scrape metrics from Armada for monitoring purposes. This may involve adding annotations to the deployment.
```bash
kubectl annotate deployment armada --namespace armada prometheus.io/scrape=true prometheus.io/port=8080
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Armada release from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall armada --namespace armada
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Armada.
```bash
kubectl delete crd armadajobs.armada.gresearch.io
kubectl delete crd armadaschedules.armada.gresearch.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Armada to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace armada
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and any important data.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace armada -o yaml > armada-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Armada release
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Armada release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade armada g-research/armada --version 0.20.36 --namespace armada
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods after the upgrade to ensure everything is functioning correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace armada
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Armada service is not reachable, ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the endpoints.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace armada
kubectl describe svc armada --namespace armada
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits set for the deployment.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment armada --namespace armada
```

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus metrics not scraping
troubleshooting.description:
If Prometheus is not scraping metrics, verify the annotations on the Armada deployment and ensure Prometheus is configured to scrape the correct port.
```bash
kubectl get deployment armada --namespace armada -o yaml | grep annotations
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Armada will have all pods in the 'Running' state and the service accessible. You should also see metrics being scraped by Prometheus if configured correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add g-research https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update
kubectl annotate deployment armada --namespace armada prometheus.io/scrape=true prometheus.io/port=8080

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and Helm installed.
